steps.title: Interpret the sediment
steps.content: Most of the sediment is fine-grained mudstone, which settles slowly in low-flow water and suggests a prolonged lake environment. Cracking from episodic desiccation shows that the lake sometimes dried, while coarse sandstone intervals indicate periods of flowing water.

steps.title: Match all three clues
steps.content: B says the lake did not dry, contradicting the cracks. C makes streams long-lasting and the lake brief, which conflicts with mostly mudstone. D says dryness prevented a lake, contradicting the low-flow-water evidence. The answer is A.
